trivia_bullets.text: Aetherdrift (DFT, released February 14, 2025) is the first Magic set to heavily foreground both Mount and Vehicle creature types in the same environment. Guidelight Matrix serves as a set-support artifact that bridges both: it can manually saddle a Mount or crew a Vehicle on command. This dual-activation design reflects the Grand Prix racing event's reliance on both creature-mounts and piloted vehicles. [WebSearch: magic.wizards.com Aetherdrift Mechanics; Scryfall: DFT 233]

Guidelight Matrix is a 2-mana artifact with three lines: (1) draw a card on entry, (2) {2},{T}: target Mount you control becomes saddled until end of turn (sorcery speed), (3) {2},{T}: target Vehicle you control becomes an artifact creature until end of turn. EDHREC's Aetherdrift artifact review called it their favorite lower-rarity card in the set, praising 'the ability to turn any of them or a Vehicle into a beater for a puny two mana' alongside the entry-trigger card draw. [EDHREC: Aetherdrift Set Review — Artifacts and Lands, via WebFetch]

Illustrated by Eli Minaya. Guidelight Matrix (no. 233) is the highest collector number of three adjacent colorless artifact commons in Aetherdrift: Aetherjacket (no. 230), Camera Launcher (no. 232), and Guidelight Matrix (no. 233). While Aetherjacket and Camera Launcher are artifact creatures, Guidelight Matrix is a non-creature artifact — the three together span the artifact-support commons of the set. [Card frontmatter: artist, collector_number; WebSearch: scryfall.com/sets/dft]
